info.name: Hiveboard Task 15 - Full Task Context Assembly Verification
info.description:
Postman collection for manually verifying Task 15 acceptance criteria from IMPLEMENTATION-GUIDE.md.

Recommended setup:
1. Start the API in Development mode so EF Core migrations run automatically.
2. Set HIVEBOARD_ADMIN_KEY before starting the API so it matches the adminApiKey collection variable.
3. Run the requests top-to-bottom in a single Runner pass; the collection registers its own worker, seeds an isolated project + epic + parent task + focal task + dependency tasks + subtask + decision per run.

Validates the five Task 15 acceptance criteria:
- AC1 — GET /tasks/{id} returns the complete nested response shape from PRD section 5.4 (task, project, epic, parentTask, subtasks, dependencies, notes, events, relatedDecisions).
- AC2 — All relationships are populated when the focal task has them: epic + parent + subtasks (with assignedAgentName) + blockedBy/blocking + notes (with agentType) + events + relatedDecisions.
- AC3 — No N+1 query issues. This is best verified by enabling EF Core 'Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Database.Command' Information logging and watching that the SELECT count is bounded and stable across calls. The 'AC3 — N+1 stability probe' folder fires the endpoint twice with extra notes in between so you can compare SQL log lines side-by-side.
- AC4 — GET /agents/me for a worker returns identity, currently assigned tasks, and unacknowledgedNotificationCount that decreases when notifications are acknowledged.
- AC5 — Null relationships (epic/parent/subtasks/dependencies/notes/events/decisions) are returned as null/[] rather than throwing.

request.description: Quick sanity check before the task-specific verification flow.
Resolves the coordinator's organization id so the rest of the collection can register isolated worker agents.

exec: pm.test('Worker has an id, plaintext API key, and a name', function () {

request.description: Registers the worker that will receive task assignments, write a note, and own a subtask. Plaintext API key is captured for subsequent worker-authenticated calls.

exec: pm.test('Focal task starts in backlog with epic + parent set', function () {

Add Dependency: Focal Blocked By Predecessor
Add Dependency: Successor Blocked By Focal

exec: pm.test('Predecessor is now done so focal can move out of backlog', function () {

item.name: Coordinator Assigns Focal Task to Worker

exec: pm.test('Focal task is now assigned to the worker', function () {

request.description: Creates an 'assigned' event + a TaskAssigned notification for the worker. Both surface in the Task 15 context bundle and in /agents/me.

exec: pm.test('Note shape includes agent name + agent type', function () {
pm.test('Focal task is now in-progress', function () {
pm.test('One backlog subtask was created', function () {
pm.test('Subtask is assigned to the worker (so subtasks[].assignedAgentName surfaces)', function () {

item.name: Coordinator Records Decision Linked to Focal Task
AC1 + AC2 — Full Nested Response & All Relationships

exec: pm.test('AC1 — top-level keys match PRD section 5.4 shape', function () {
pm.test('AC1 — task block has core fields populated', function () {
pm.test('AC2 — project is populated (new in Task 15)', function () {
pm.test('AC2 — epic is populated (id, title, description, status)', function () {
pm.test('AC2 — subtasks include the assigned subtask with assignedAgentName (new in Task 15)', function () {
pm.test('AC2 — dependencies.blockedBy includes the done predecessor', function () {
pm.test('AC2 — dependencies.blocking includes the backlog successor', function () {
pm.test('AC2 — notes include the worker context note with agent + agentType (new in Task 15)', function () {
pm.test('AC2 — events include assigned + status_changed + note_added + decomposed', function () {
pm.test('AC2 — events are ordered chronologically (ascending timestamp)', function () {
pm.test('AC2 — relatedDecisions includes the JWT decision', function () {

request.description: AC1 + AC2 — pulls the full context bundle for the focal task and asserts every relationship the PRD section 5.4 calls out, including the new fields introduced by Task 15 (project, subtasks[].assignedAgentName, notes[].agentType).

item.name: AC5 — Null Relationships Are Handled Gracefully
GET /tasks/{bareTaskId} — No Epic, No Parent, No Notes

exec: pm.test('AC5 — task block is still populated for the bare task', function () {
pm.test('AC5 — project block is required and still populated', function () {
pm.test('AC5 — collections default to empty arrays, not null', function () {

request.description: AC5 — verifies that nullable relationships (epic, parent, subtasks, dependencies, notes, events, decisions) are returned as null/[] rather than throwing.
Confirms the lookup short-circuits cleanly with 404 instead of producing a malformed context bundle.

item.name: AC4 — /agents/me Includes Assigned Tasks + Notification Count

exec: pm.test('AC4 — agent identity (id, name, type, platform, status)', function () {
pm.test('AC4 — assignedTasks is an array of {id,title,status,projectId,updatedAt}', function () {
pm.test('AC4 — assignedTasks contains the focal task and subtask', function () {
pm.test('AC4 — assignedTasks excludes done tasks (predecessor)', function () {
pm.test('AC4 — assignedTasks status is lower-case', function () {
pm.test('AC4 — unacknowledgedNotificationCount is a number >= 1 (TaskAssigned was created)', function () {

request.description: AC4 — verifies the enriched /agents/me payload (assignedTasks list + unacknowledgedNotificationCount).

exec: pm.test('Worker has at least one unacknowledged notification', function () {

request.description: Pulls the first unacknowledged notification id so the next step can ack it and prove that unacknowledgedNotificationCount drops by exactly 1.

exec: pm.test('Acknowledged notification id matches and is marked acknowledged', function () {
pm.test('AC4 — unacknowledgedNotificationCount decreased by exactly 1 after ack', function () {

request.description: AC4 — proves that unacknowledgedNotificationCount is sourced from the live notification table by acknowledging one entry and re-fetching.

item.name: AC3 — N+1 Stability Probe (Manual Log Inspection)

exec: // AC3 cannot be asserted programmatically from Postman — it requires inspecting EF Core SQL logs.
// To verify: enable 'Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Database.Command' Information logging in
// src/Hiveboard.Api/appsettings.Development.json before this run, then count SELECTs emitted
// for this call. Expect ~8–10 (one per Include path) when AsSplitQuery() is active.

item.name: Second Probe — Same Endpoint, More Children

exec: pm.test('Second probe sees both notes — confirms data did grow between calls', function () {
// Compare the SELECT count emitted for this call against the first probe.
// AC3 passes when the count is identical (no per-row queries fired against Notes/Agents).

request.description: AC3 — second N+1 probe call. Compare the SQL log between this and the first probe; the SELECT count should be identical even though the focal task now has more notes/events.
Confirms the endpoint rejects unauthenticated requests so the new context bundle does not leak data outside an org.
